'''周参見王子神社'''は[[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]にある神社。
==概要== [[1546年|1546]]（[[天文|天文15年]]）に当時の周参見領主、左衛門太夫藤原氏安によって現在地に若一王子として建立された。江戸末期から明治にかけて奉納された多数の絵馬は、すさみ町文化財に指定され、境内にある歴史民俗資料館に保存・展示されている。なお、王子社を名乗るが、[[九十九王子|熊野九十九王子社]]には数えられない。
*所在地:[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]周参見山崎2290番地 *交通機関：[[JR]][[紀勢本線]][[周参見駅]]
==すさみ町立歴史民俗資料館== 周参見王子神社境内にある。すさみ町の生活・産業資料や古文書、周参見王子神社奉納絵馬など歴史的・民俗的資史料を保存・展示している。開館は火・土曜日の午前9時～午後4時。入館無料。
==関連項目== *[[大辺路]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ 歴史民俗資料館（町教育委員会）]
[[Category:神社|すさみおうししんしや]] [[Category:神道|すさみおうししんしや]] [[Category:大辺路の王子社|すさみおうししんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の建築物・観光名所|すさみおうししんしや]]
{{shinto-stub}}

==概要== [[1546年|1546]]（[[天文|天文15年]]）に当時の周参見領主、左衛門太夫藤原氏安によって現在地に若一王子として建立された。江戸末期から明治にかけて奉納された多数の絵馬は、すさみ町文化財に指定され、境内にある歴史民俗資料館に保存・展示されている。なお、王子を名乗るが、[[九十九王子|熊野九十九王子社]]には数えられない。
*所在地:[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]周参見山崎2290番地 *交通機関：[[JR]][[紀勢本線]][[周参見駅]]
==すさみ町立歴史民俗資料館== 周参見王子神社境内にある。すさみ町の生活・産業資料や古文書、周参見王子神社奉納絵馬など歴史的・民俗的資史料を保存・展示している。開館は火・土曜日の午前9時～午後4時。入館無料。

{{shinto-stub}}

'''周参見王子神社'''は[[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]にある神社。
==概要== [[1546年|1546]]（[[天文|天文15年]]）に当時の周参見領主、左衛門太夫藤原氏安によって現在地に建立された。
[[江戸時代]]末期から[[明治]]にかけて奉納された絵馬55点が遺されている。そのうち約6割が船絵馬であって、周参見に船の出入りが多かったことを伝えている。これらは町文化財に指定され、境内にある歴史民俗資料館に保存・展示されている。
『[[紀伊続風土記]]』によれば、当初は「若一（にゃくいち）王子権現社」の名であったという。このことから、熊野から勧請されて建立された神社であることが分かるが、成立の年代や経緯から、[[九十九王子|熊野九十九王子社]]には数えられない。
*所在地:[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]周参見山崎2290番地 *交通機関：[[JR]][[紀勢本線]][[周参見駅]]
==すさみ町立歴史民俗資料館== 周参見王子神社境内にある。すさみ町の生活・産業資料や古文書、周参見王子神社奉納絵馬など、歴史的・民俗的資史料を保存・展示している。開館は火・土曜日の午前9時～午後4時。入館無料。
==関連項目== *[[大辺路]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ 歴史民俗資料館（町教育委員会）]
[[Category:神社|すさみおうししんしや]] [[Category:神道|すさみおうししんしや]] <!--[[Category:大辺路の王子社|すさみおうししんしや]]--> [[Category:和歌山県の神社|すさみおうししんしや]]
{{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
'''周参見王子神社'''（すさみおうじじんじゃ）は[[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]にある神社。
==概要== [[1546年|1546]]（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年）に当時の周参見領主、左衛門太夫藤原氏安によって現在地に建立された。
[[江戸時代]]末期から[[明治]]にかけて奉納された絵馬55点が遺されている。そのうち約6割が船絵馬であって、周参見に船の出入りが多かったことを伝えている。これらは町文化財に指定され、境内にある歴史民俗資料館に保存・展示されている。
『[[紀伊続風土記]]』によれば、当初は「若一（にゃくいち）王子権現社」の名であったという。このことから、熊野から勧請されて建立された神社であることが分かるが、成立の年代や経緯から、[[九十九王子|熊野九十九王子社]]には数えられない。
*所在地:[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]周参見山崎2290番地 *交通機関：[[JR]][[紀勢本線]][[周参見駅]]
==すさみ町立歴史民俗資料館== 周参見王子神社境内にある。すさみ町の生活・産業資料や古文書、周参見王子神社奉納絵馬など、歴史的・民俗的資史料を保存・展示している。開館は火・土曜日の午前9時～午後4時。入館無料。
==関連項目== *[[大辺路]] *[[大辺路の王子社一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ 歴史民俗資料館（町教育委員会）]
{{DEFAULTSORT:すさみおうししんしや}} [[Category:和歌山県の神社]] [[Category:すさみ町]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''周参見王子神社'''（すさみおうじじんじゃ）は[[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]にある神社。
== 概要 == [[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（[[1546年]]）に当時の周参見領主、左衛門太夫藤原氏安によって現在地に建立された。
[[江戸時代]]末期から[[明治]]にかけて奉納された絵馬55点が遺されている。そのうち約6割が船絵馬であって、周参見に船の出入りが多かったことを伝えている。これらは町文化財に指定され、境内にある歴史民俗資料館に保存・展示されている。
『[[紀伊続風土記]]』によれば、当初は「若一（にゃくいち）王子権現社」の名であったという。このことから、熊野から勧請されて建立された神社であることが分かるが、成立の年代や経緯から、[[九十九王子|熊野九十九王子社]]には数えられない。

『[[紀伊続風土記]]』によれば、当初は「若一（にゃくいち）王子権現社」の名であったという。このことから、熊野から勧請されて建立された神社であることが分かるが、成立の年代や経緯から、[[九十九王子|熊野九十九王子社]]には数えられない。

==すさみ町立歴史民俗資料館== [[ファイル:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|サムネイル|すさみ町立歴史民俗資料館]] 周参見王子神社境内にある。すさみ町の生活・産業資料や古文書、周参見王子神社奉納絵馬など、歴史的・民俗的資史料を保存・展示している。開館は火・土曜日の午前9時～午後4時。入館無料。
==関連項目== *[[大辺路]] *[[大辺路の王子社一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ 歴史民俗資料館（町教育委員会）] {{神道 横}} {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|10.4|N|135|29|40.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すさみおうししんしや}} [[Category:和歌山県の神社]] [[Category:すさみ町の建築物]] [[Category:すさみ町の歴史]] [[Category:熊野信仰]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{Shrine |Name = Susami Ōji Shrine |Image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |Location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |Shrine Rank = |Founded = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate Name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |Annual Festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.
* Location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: making drafts attribute to me
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Susami Ōji Shrine |Image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |Location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |Shrine Rank = |Founded = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate Name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |Annual Festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.
* Location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

{{Shrine |name = Susami Ōji Shrine |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |type = |consecrated = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |Deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |type = |consecration_year = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

{{Shrine |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |type = |consecration_year = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |type = |consecration_year = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |type = |consecration_year = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |type = |consecration_year = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |type = |consecration_year = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |type = |consecration_year = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |type = |consecration_year = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |type = |consecration_year = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |type = |consecration_year = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |type = |consecration_year = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free.
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神}} |type = |consecration_year = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町|12=draft}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} |type = |consecration_year = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)|12=draft}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年|12=draft}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)|12=draft}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年|12=draft}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu|12=draft}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬|12=draft}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=draft}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野|12=draft}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|12=draft}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町|12=draft}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|12=draft}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

21 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:周参見王子神社 up to rev 98828363
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 周参見王子神社 |画像 = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]周参見山崎2290番地 |祭神 = [[天照大神|天照大御神]] |社格 = |創建 = [[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（[[1546年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 王子神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = }} '''周参見王子神社'''（すさみおうじじんじゃ）は[[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]にある神社。
== 概要 == [[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（[[1546年]]）に当時の周参見領主、左衛門太夫[[周参見氏|藤原氏安]]によって現在地に建立された。
[[江戸時代]]末期から[[明治]]にかけて奉納された絵馬55点が遺されている。そのうち約6割が船絵馬であって、周参見に船の出入りが多かったことを伝えている。これらは町文化財に指定され、境内にある歴史民俗資料館に保存・展示されている。
『[[紀伊続風土記]]』によれば、当初は「若一（にゃくいち）王子権現社」の名であったという。このことから、熊野から勧請されて建立された神社であることが分かるが、成立の年代や経緯から、[[九十九王子|熊野九十九王子社]]には数えられない。
* 所在地:[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]周参見山崎2290番地 * 交通機関：[[JR]][[紀勢本線]][[周参見駅]]
==すさみ町立歴史民俗資料館== [[ファイル:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|サムネイル|すさみ町立歴史民俗資料館]] 周参見王子神社境内にある。すさみ町の生活・産業資料や古文書、周参見王子神社奉納絵馬など、歴史的・民俗的資史料を保存・展示している。開館は火・土曜日の午前9時～午後4時。入館無料。
==関連項目== *[[大辺路]] *[[大辺路の王子社一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ 歴史民俗資料館（町教育委員会）] {{神道 横}} {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|10.4|N|135|29|40.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すさみおうししんしや}} [[Category:和歌山県の神社]] [[Category:すさみ町の建築物]] [[Category:すさみ町の歴史]] [[Category:熊野信仰]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[周参見王子神社]] to [[Susami-ōji Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 周参見王子神社 |画像 = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]周参見山崎2290番地 |祭神 = [[天照大神|天照大御神]] |社格 = |創建 = [[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（[[1546年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 王子神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = }} '''周参見王子神社'''（すさみおうじじんじゃ）は[[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]にある神社。
== 概要 == [[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（[[1546年]]）に当時の周参見領主、左衛門太夫[[周参見氏|藤原氏安]]によって現在地に建立された。
[[江戸時代]]末期から[[明治]]にかけて奉納された絵馬55点が遺されている。そのうち約6割が船絵馬であって、周参見に船の出入りが多かったことを伝えている。これらは町文化財に指定され、境内にある歴史民俗資料館に保存・展示されている。
『[[紀伊続風土記]]』によれば、当初は「若一（にゃくいち）王子権現社」の名であったという。このことから、熊野から勧請されて建立された神社であることが分かるが、成立の年代や経緯から、[[九十九王子|熊野九十九王子社]]には数えられない。
* 所在地:[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]周参見山崎2290番地 * 交通機関：[[JR]][[紀勢本線]][[周参見駅]]
==すさみ町立歴史民俗資料館== [[ファイル:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|サムネイル|すさみ町立歴史民俗資料館]] 周参見王子神社境内にある。すさみ町の生活・産業資料や古文書、周参見王子神社奉納絵馬など、歴史的・民俗的資史料を保存・展示している。開館は火・土曜日の午前9時～午後4時。入館無料。
==関連項目== *[[大辺路]] *[[大辺路の王子社一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ 歴史民俗資料館（町教育委員会）] {{神道 横}} {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|33|10.4|N|135|29|40.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すさみおうししんしや}} [[Category:和歌山県の神社]] [[Category:すさみ町の建築物]] [[Category:すさみ町の歴史]] [[Category:熊野信仰]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishimuro District, Wakayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susami Town|qq=}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu Omikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} |type = |consecration_year = {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (Japanese era)|qq=}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1546|qq=}}) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susami Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|ja|西牟婁郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishimuro District, Wakayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenmon (Japanese era)|ja|天文 (元号)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (Japanese era)|qq=}} 15 ({{ill|1546|ja|1546年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1546|qq=}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara Shiyasu|qq=}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|ja|絵馬|1=Ema (Shinto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema (Shinto)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema (Shinto)|qq=}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano|ja|熊野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano|qq=}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Kumano Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami Town|ja|すさみ町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susami Town|qq=}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|ja|周参見駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susami Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisei Main Line|qq=}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] |type = |consecration_year = [[:en:Tenbun|Tenmon (Japanese era)]] 15 ([[:en:1546|1546]]) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The shrine was established in [[:en:Tenbun|Tenmon (Japanese era)]] 15 ([[:en:1546|1546]]) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara Shiyasu|qq=}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] (votive plaques) from the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] to the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]], but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Kumano Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.
* location: [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near [[:en:Susami Station|Susami Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] |type = |consecration_year = [[:en:Tenbun|Tenmon (Japanese era)]] 15 ([[:en:1546|1546]]) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = }} '''Susami Ōji Shrine''' (すさみおうじじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The shrine was established in [[:en:Tenbun|Tenmon (Japanese era)]] 15 ([[:en:1546|1546]]) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara Shiyasu|qq=}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] (votive plaques) from the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] to the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]], but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.
* location: [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near [[:en:Susami Station|Susami Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11416878]] Wikidata name: Susami-ōji Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] |type = |consecration_year = [[:en:Tenbun|Tenmon (Japanese era)]] 15 ([[:en:1546|1546]]) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = |prefecture = {{ill|Susami|qid=Q1346937}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.552889|N|135.4945|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Susami Ōji Shrine'''|周参見王子神社}} is a shrine located in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The shrine was established in [[:en:Tenbun|Tenmon (Japanese era)]] 15 ([[:en:1546|1546]]) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara Shiyasu|qq=}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] (votive plaques) from the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] to the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]], but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.
* location: [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near [[:en:Susami Station|Susami Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

* location: [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near [[:en:Susami Station|Susami Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] (JR).

The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}

Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|qq=|1=Ōhechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2748597}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11416878]] Wikidata name: Susami-ōji Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] |type = |consecration_year = [[:en:Tenbun|Tenmon (Japanese era)]] 15 ([[:en:1546|1546]]) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = |location = {{ill|Susami|qid=Q1346937}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.552889|N|135.4945|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Susami Ōji Shrine'''|周参見王子神社}} is a shrine located in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The shrine was established in [[:en:Tenbun|Tenmon (Japanese era)]] 15 ([[:en:1546|1546]]) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara Shiyasu|qq=|1=Fujihara Shiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416877}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] (votive plaques) from the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] to the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]], but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.
* location: [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near [[:en:Susami Station|Susami Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|qq=|1=Ōhechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2748597}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:周参見王子神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11416878]] Wikidata name: Susami-ōji Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] |type = |consecration_year = [[:en:Tenbun|Tenmon (Japanese era)]] 15 ([[:en:1546|1546]]) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = |location = {{ill|Susami|WD=Q1346937}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.552889|N|135.4945|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Susami Ōji Shrine'''|周参見王子神社}} is a shrine located in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The shrine was established in [[:en:Tenbun|Tenmon (Japanese era)]] 15 ([[:en:1546|1546]]) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara Shiyasu|qq=|1=Fujihara Shiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416877}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] (votive plaques) from the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] to the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]], but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.
* location: [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near [[:en:Susami Station|Susami Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|qq=|1=Ōhechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2748597}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Susami-ōji Shrine" to "Susami Ōji Shrine"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:周参見王子神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11416878]] Wikidata name: Susami Ōji Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susami Ōji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Susami-Oji Shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], Susami Yamazaki 2290 |deity = [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] |type = |consecration_year = [[:en:Tenbun|Tenmon (Japanese era)]] 15 ([[:en:1546|1546]]) |Main Hall = |Alternate name = Ōji Shrine |Pilgrimage Site = |festival = |Rituals = |Map = |location = {{ill|Susami|WD=Q1346937}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.552889|N|135.4945|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{nihongo|'''Susami Ōji Shrine'''|周参見王子神社}} is a shrine located in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== Overview == The shrine was established in [[:en:Tenbun|Tenmon (Japanese era)]] 15 ([[:en:1546|1546]]) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara Shiyasu|1=Fujihara Shiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416877}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] (votive plaques) from the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] to the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]], but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.
* location: [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near [[:en:Susami Station|Susami Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|1=Ōhechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2748597}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]], but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.
* location: [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami Town]], Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near [[:en:Susami Station|Susami Station]] on the [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|1=Ōhechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2748597}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}
