This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami, Wakayama|en|Susami, Wakayama|WD=Q1346937|lt=Susami Town}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|en|Susami Station|WD=Q2748626|lt=Susami Station}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|1=Ōhechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2748597}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}

== Overview == The shrine was established in {{ill|Tenbun|en|Tenbun|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenmon (Japanese era)}} 15 ({{ill|1546|en|1546|WD=Q6533|lt=1546}}) by the local lord of Susami at the time, Saemon no Daifu {{ill|Fujihara Shiyasu|ja|周参見氏|lt=Fujiwara Shiyasu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara Shiyasu|1=Fujihara Shiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416877}}, at its current location.
Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=Ema (Shinto)}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.

The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|1=Ōhechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2748597}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}

Fifty-five {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=Ema (Shinto)|1=Ema (Shinto)}} (votive plaques) from the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} have been preserved. About 60% of these are votive ship plaques, reflecting the high maritime traffic in Susami. These ema are designated as town cultural properties and are stored and displayed in the Historical and Folklore Museum located within the shrine grounds.
According to the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, the shrine was initially named "Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen Shrine" (若一王子権現社). This indicates that the shrine was established as a branch shrine of {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano|1=Kumano region}}, but due to its founding date and background, it is not counted among the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}.
* location: {{ill|Susami, Wakayama|en|Susami, Wakayama|WD=Q1346937|lt=Susami Town|1=Susami, Wakayama}}, Susami Yamazaki 2290 * Transportation: Near {{ill|Susami Station|en|Susami Station|WD=Q2748626|lt=Susami Station|1=Susami Station}} on the {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line|1=Kisei Main Line}} (JR).
== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|1=Ōhechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2748597}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.susami.lg.jp/kyouiku/rekisi/siryokan/ Historical and Folklore Museum (Town Board of Education)]

== Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore == [[File:Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|thumb|Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore]] Located on the grounds of Susami Ōji Shrine, the Susami Municipal Museum of History and Folklore preserves and exhibits historical and folkloric materials, including artifacts related to the daily life and industries of Susami, old documents, and the ema dedicated to Susami Ōji Shrine. The museum is open on Tuesdays and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Admission is free. == See Also == * {{ill|Ōheji|ja|大辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōheji|1=Ōhechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2748597}} * {{ill|List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|ja|大辺路の王子社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ōji Shrines along Ōheji|qq=}}
